prompt: Design a HarborStone Listings Operations Suite to manage an offline real-estate listing catalog and its internal publishing workflow for US brokerage teams. The system serves four roles: Regular User (agent/assistant) who drafts and uploads listing content, Merchant (brokerage office) who owns inventory and approves final publish, Operations who curates promotions and monitors KPIs, and Administrator who manages access policies and risk controls. The Vue.js frontend provides a desktop-first workspace where users sign in with a local username and password, then create or edit listings with guided data entry for price, living area, beds/baths, floor level, orientation, and optional manually-entered coordinates. Users can attach up to 25 files per listing, including photos, short walkthrough videos, and PDFs; the UI must validate formats and sizes before upload (JPEG/PNG/WebP up to 12 MB each, MP4 up to 200 MB, PDF up to 20 MB), show progress, and clearly indicate when a file is rejected for invalid type, oversize, or duplicate. Operations users get an analytics dashboard showing daily and monthly KPIs (new users, active users, listings published, engagement actions, and a simple conversion funnel from draft to approved to published), with exports to CSV for a selected date range and filters by office and agent. Promo slots allow Operations to pin up to 20 listings per thematic collection (for example “Open Houses 03/27/2026”) with start/end times and a manual rank order, and the UI must reflect scheduled visibility changes without requiring internet connectivity. The backend uses Koa to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js app, and MySQL persists users, roles, listings, attachments, promo collections, KPI aggregates, audit events, and risk profiles. Authentication is local-only using salted password hashing (minimum 12 characters, must include upper, lower, number, and symbol), with JWT sessions that expire after 30 minutes of inactivity and rotate on privilege changes; replay protection is enforced via short-lived nonces (5-minute TTL) and idempotency keys for write actions. Rate limiting blocks brute force attempts (e.g., 10 login attempts per username per 15 minutes) and throttles high-volume APIs; optional offline-friendly CAPTCHA can be enabled for suspicious traffic patterns. Attachment handling includes server-side re-validation, image compression to a max 2048 px long edge at 85% quality, SHA-256 deduplication per listing, EXIF stripping for photos, and version retention of the last 5 revisions per file with the ability to roll back; object storage is abstracted behind a local filesystem repository so deployments can choose a mounted drive while keeping a consistent API. Data cleansing runs on every listing save: it normalizes currency to USD with two decimals, converts area inputs between sqft and sqm, standardizes layouts (e.g., “2 bed 1 bath”), applies missing-value rules (reject publish if price or area is blank), flags anomalies (price per sqft outside configurable thresholds such as $50–$5,000), and applies rule-based corrections (trim whitespace, normalize orientation values). Security and compliance are enforced with HTTPS in local networks via installable certificates, CSRF/XSS/injection protections, field-level masking for sensitive values in logs, tiered access control on every endpoint, and immutable audit logs capturing before/after values, operator, timestamp, and IP for publish/approve, role changes, deletes, and promo edits. Privacy consent is recorded on first login, and retention policies auto-delete soft-deleted listings after 90 days while keeping audit logs for 1 year unless an Administrator executes an immediate purge. Exception and risk controls maintain blacklists and a credit score that decays over time, incrementing for repeated no-show approvals, policy violations, or abnormal multi-device login patterns, with configurable actions such as forced password reset and temporary lockouts.
